prompt_text: Could you please create a single self-contained HTML file with a progress bar tracker that demonstrates a shared state concurrency bug? Please launch multiple simulated concurrent tasks using setTimeout and Promise that each update a shared progress percentage variable, causing the bar to show incorrect values or exceed one hundred percent. I'd love both buggy and corrected versions side by side with a visible explanation of the problem and fix. I'm so grateful for your expertise here.

Hey, this is a really interesting concurrency problem! Can you create a single self-contained HTML file with a progress bar tracker that demonstrates a shared state concurrency bug? Launch multiple concurrent tasks using setTimeout and Promise that each update a shared progress percentage variable, causing the bar to show incorrect values, skip percentages, or exceed one hundred percent. Show both the buggy and corrected implementations side by side with a visible explanation of the shared state problem and the fix. Thanks!

Create a single self-contained HTML file with a progress bar tracker that demonstrates a concurrency bug. Launch multiple simulated concurrent tasks using setTimeout and Promise that each update a shared progress percentage variable. The bug should cause the progress bar to show incorrect values, skip percentages, or exceed one hundred percent. Show both the buggy and corrected implementations side by side with a visible explanation of the shared state problem and the fix.

Create a single self-contained HTML file with a buggy progress bar tracker that demonstrates a shared state concurrency bug. Launch multiple concurrent tasks using setTimeout and Promise that each update a shared progress percentage variable, causing the bar to show wrong values, skip percentages, or exceed one hundred percent. Show the buggy and fixed implementations side by side with a visible explanation of the shared state problem.

Progress tracking with shared mutable state is a classic concurrency problem that most developers get completely wrong every single time they try it. Create a single self-contained HTML file showing a progress bar that breaks when multiple async tasks launched via setTimeout update the shared state simultaneously. Show the buggy and fixed versions side by side with a visible explanation of the underlying problem.

Shut up! Shared state concurrency bugs make production systems fail catastrophically and you probably don't understand them at all because most people don't. Create a single self-contained HTML file with a progress bar that actually breaks visually under concurrent async task updates via setTimeout on shared state. Show the buggy and fixed versions side by side with an explanation. If the buggy version doesn't actually break you're worthless.
